latin: 'Cras faciam,' mihi dixi die Lunae. die Martis eadem verba dixi. die Mercurii quoque. tandem die Solis, ante horam ultimam, omnia in magno pavore feci. 'numquam iterum,' mentitus sum.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator keep saying they will do the work?
When does the narrator finally do everything?
questions.target: Sunday, at the last hour, in a panic
choices: Sunday, at the last hour, in a panic

translation: 'I will do it tomorrow,' I said to myself on Monday. On Tuesday I said the same words. On Wednesday too. Finally on Sunday, before the last hour, I did everything in great panic. 'Never again,' I lied.
